stories.title: Fuel supply is back above pre-Iran-war levels, but the excise cut question remains open
stories.summary: Australia says it now has more petrol, diesel and jet fuel on hand than it did before the Iran war began on February 28. The political pressure point is June 30, when the temporary fuel excise cut is due to expire.

links.headline: Fuel stocks recover as excise deadline nears
links.excerpt: Public broadcaster coverage focused on supply levels, government messaging and the next political deadline.
links.imageAlt: Fuel stocks recover as excise deadline nears

links.headline: Jim Chalmers rules out fuel excise extension and downplays hopes for tax relief in ‘most responsible’ budget yet
links.excerpt: The treasurer also says May federal budget will play ‘helpful not harmful role in fight against inflation’
links.imageAlt: Jim Chalmers rules out fuel excise extension and downplays hopes for tax relief in ‘most responsible’ budget yet

links.headline: Fuel excise halved for three months
links.excerpt: Following a meeting of the National Cabinet today convened by the Prime Minister, the Australian Government will halve the fuel excise on petrol and diesel for three months.The halving of the fuel excise will reduce the cost of fuel by 26.3 cents per litre.This will reduce the cost of a 65L tank of fuel by nearly $19.The spike in fuel prices as a result of the war in the Middle East is hurting Australians and causing financial stress. This will help to provide some relief.The halving of the fuel excise will commence from April 1 and run to 30 June.

stories.imageAlt: Fuel stocks recover as excise deadline nears

stories.title: Widespread rain and the first north-west cloudband of 2026
stories.summary: The main weather story is a broad rain band from the Kimberley through central Australia and into the south-east, with possible drought relief but also local disruption. Specialist and official weather pages are better than partisan news on this one.

links.headline: First north-west cloudband of 2026 to soak all states
links.excerpt: The most widespread May rain in a decade is about to sweep across Australia as the first north-west cloudband of 2026 becomes a reality.
links.imageAlt: First north-west cloudband of 2026 to soak all states

links.headline: Weather - Australia 7 Day Forecasts & Weather Radar
links.excerpt: Weather forecasts, current conditions, weather radar and warnings from the Bureau of Meteorology plus world weather, surf and snow reports.

stories.imageAlt: First north-west cloudband of 2026 to soak all states

stories.title: Airline passenger rights overhaul gets budget funding
stories.summary: The government has funded a new aviation consumer protection authority and ombuds scheme, with compensation possible for disrupted passengers. Consumer groups like the direction; airlines warn the rules could push up fares or reduce route viability.

links.headline: $40m bid for airline crackdown with warnings of fare rises, cancelled flights
links.excerpt: The federal government has announced nearly $40 million to fund an overhaul of aviation consumer rights.
links.imageAlt: $40m bid for airline crackdown with warnings of fare rises, cancelled flights

links.headline: Budget flags $40m+ for airline competition and consumer protection
links.excerpt: In the 2026 Budget, announced this week, $38.1 million over four years from 2026–27 and $7.3 million per year ongoing was earmarked for the government’s consumer protection priorities, as well as $4.5 million over four years to continue the ACCC’s domestic airline monitoring program.
links.imageAlt: Budget flags $40m+ for airline competition and consumer protection

stories.imageAlt: $40m bid for airline crackdown with warnings of fare rises, cancelled flights

stories.title: Diphtheria outbreak and first reported death in nearly a decade
stories.summary: The NT death has pushed a rare disease back into national attention. Cases are concentrated in remote and regional communities, particularly Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities, with authorities pointing to vaccine coverage and transmission risks.

links.headline: Diphtheria is back in Australia. Here's what to know
links.excerpt: Once a major cause of childhood deaths, diphtheria was largely eliminated through vaccination. Health authorities say falling immunity may now be helping the disease spread again.
links.imageAlt: Diphtheria is back in Australia. Here's what to know

links.headline: Michele Bullock says ‘we are poorer and there is no way out of it’ after rate rise – as it happened
links.excerpt: This blog is now closed
links.imageAlt: Michele Bullock says ‘we are poorer and there is no way out of it’ after rate rise – as it happened

stories.imageAlt: Diphtheria is back in Australia. Here's what to know

stories.title: Chinese undersea monitoring device and Australia's submarine problem
stories.summary: A device found near the Lombok Strait has revived concerns about Chinese undersea surveillance, submarine detection, and how AUKUS-era Australia should balance crewed submarines with undersea drones and sensors.

links.headline: Chinese undersea device found near Bali may be part of something bigger
links.excerpt: The discovery of a Chinese undersea monitoring device in a strategic waterway "shows the extent of both China's reach and its undersea ambitions" and has implications for Australia's defence strategy, say experts.
links.imageAlt: Chinese undersea device found near Bali may be part of something bigger

links.headline: Chinese device found in strategic waters near Bali identified
links.excerpt: A large torpedo-shaped device discovered near an island in a strategically important strait between Bali and Lombok has been identified by defence analysts as a Chinese undersea monitoring system.
links.imageAlt: Chinese device found in strategic waters near Bali identified

stories.imageAlt: Chinese undersea device found near Bali may be part of something bigger

stories.title: Budget fight: bracket creep, migration and housing
stories.summary: The Coalition is pushing permanent tax-bracket indexation and tighter links between migration and housing supply. The costings and visa-class details are the pressure points, with universities and skilled-migration users likely to be in the firing line.

links.headline: Coalition proposes plan to end tax bracket creep
links.excerpt: The Coalition has made a bold play to win back voters with a plan to automatically index income tax brackets to keep up with inflation.
links.imageAlt: Coalition proposes plan to end tax bracket creep

links.headline: Coalition tax plan could cost more than suggested in first few years
links.excerpt: The Coalition's plan to index tax brackets could cost $35 billion in the first four years, more than has been suggested, and $44 billion in one year alone in 2035-36.
links.imageAlt: Coalition tax plan could cost more than suggested in first few years

links.headline: Government defends 'contentious' changes in budget as Hanson labels spending 'wrong' — as it happened
links.excerpt: Treasurer Jim Chalmers has announced major tax reforms that he said will target intergenerational inequity.
links.imageAlt: Government defends 'contentious' changes in budget as Hanson labels spending 'wrong' — as it happened

stories.imageAlt: Coalition proposes plan to end tax bracket creep

stories.title: Kyiv mourns after major Russian barrage; prisoner swap begins
stories.summary: A Russian missile strike on a Kyiv apartment block killed 24 people, including three teenagers, after days of heavy drone and missile attacks. Russia and Ukraine also swapped 205 POWs each, billed as the first stage of a planned 1,000-for-1,000 exchange.

links.headline: Kyiv mourns after deadly apartment strike
links.excerpt: Wire report with casualty updates, local reaction and broader context around Russia's latest barrage.
links.imageAlt: Kyiv mourns after deadly apartment strike

links.headline: Ukraine ‘entirely justified’ to plan strikes against Russian energy and military targets after Kyiv attacks, Zelenskyy says – as it happpened
links.excerpt: Zelenskyy accused Russia of committing war crimes after latest strikes on Ukraine killed at least 24
links.imageAlt: Ukraine ‘entirely justified’ to plan strikes against Russian energy and military targets after Kyiv attacks, Zelenskyy says – as it happpened

links.headline: Zelenskyy vows response after Russian strike kills 24 in Kyiv
links.excerpt: Ukraine’s air force says the attack on Thursday was part of the largest aerial assault since the war began.
links.imageAlt: Zelenskyy vows response after Russian strike kills 24 in Kyiv

stories.imageAlt: Kyiv mourns after deadly apartment strike

stories.title: Ukraine strikes Ryazan and Russian oil infrastructure
stories.summary: Ukraine's drone campaign against Russian energy targets continued, with Ryazan hit and Russian officials reporting deaths and damage. These strikes are intended to squeeze Russian war revenue and logistics, but civilian harm inside Russia complicates the moral and diplomatic picture.

links.headline: Ukraine attacks Russia with drones after suffering three days of massive strikes
links.excerpt: Large-scale attack on Russian regions and huge oil refinery comes after 24 were killed when missile hit flats in Kyiv
links.imageAlt: Ukraine attacks Russia with drones after suffering three days of massive strikes

links.headline: Ukrainian drones hit oil refinery in Russia's Ryazan, Ukraine says
links.imageAlt: Ukrainian drones hit oil refinery in Russia's Ryazan, Ukraine says

links.quality: Independent Russian outlet critical of Kremlin
links.headline: Ukrainian Drones Kill 4 and Set Ryazan Oil Refinery Ablaze - The Moscow Times
links.excerpt: A Ukrainian drone barrage killed at least four people and ignited a huge fire at an oil refinery in the city of Ryazan on Friday, in what appeared to be a direct retaliation for a deadly Russian strike on Kyiv a day earlier.
links.imageAlt: Ukrainian Drones Kill 4 and Set Ryazan Oil Refinery Ablaze - The Moscow Times

links.quality: Ukrainian outlet with clear pro-Ukraine perspective
links.headline: Massive fire erupts at Russia's Ryazan oil refinery as Ukrainian drone blitz hits multiple targets, oblasts
links.excerpt: Ukraine's military reportedly launched a large-scale drone attack overnight on May 15, striking Russian military and energy infrastructure in multiple regions of Russia, Russian Telegram media channels reported.
links.imageAlt: Massive fire erupts at Russia's Ryazan oil refinery as Ukrainian drone blitz hits multiple targets, oblasts

stories.imageAlt: Ukraine attacks Russia with drones after suffering three days of massive strikes

stories.title: Trump leaves Beijing without a clear Iran breakthrough
stories.summary: Trump and Xi agreed publicly that Iran should not obtain nuclear weapons and that Hormuz should reopen, but China made no concrete public commitment to help end the war. The visit looked more like a tactical thaw than a diplomatic solution.

links.headline: No major Iran war breakthroughs as Trump leaves Beijing
links.excerpt: US President Donald Trump left China on Friday without securing tangible help from Beijing to end the Iran war, but praised China's President, Xi Jinping, during his visit.
links.imageAlt: No major Iran war breakthroughs as Trump leaves Beijing

links.headline: Trump leaves China without breakthroughs on Iran, Taiwan or AI
links.excerpt: US president hails ‘fantastic’ deals, but details remain scarce after pageantry and little progress at much-hyped summit with Xi
links.imageAlt: Trump leaves China without breakthroughs on Iran, Taiwan or AI

links.headline: Trump, Xi discuss Strait of Hormuz as Chinese vessels transit key waterway
links.excerpt: White House says the two leaders agree that the strait ‘must remain open to support the free flow of energy’.
links.imageAlt: Trump, Xi discuss Strait of Hormuz as Chinese vessels transit key waterway

stories.imageAlt: No major Iran war breakthroughs as Trump leaves Beijing

stories.title: Iran says mistrust is blocking talks with the US
stories.summary: Iran's foreign minister says Tehran does not trust Washington and wants a fair and balanced deal, while Trump has dismissed Iran's latest proposal. The enriched uranium issue and Hormuz remain the hard problems.

links.headline: Iran's top diplomat says a lack of trust is impeding talks to end war with the US
links.excerpt: Iran’s foreign minister says that a lack of trust remains the biggest obstacle in negotiations to end the war with the U.S.
links.imageAlt: Iran's top diplomat says a lack of trust is impeding talks to end war with the US

links.headline: Iran warns US to stay out of Hormuz after Trump says US will ‘guide’ ships
links.excerpt: President Trump says the US will begin ‘Project Freedom’ on Monday to guide stranded ships through Strait of Hormuz.
links.imageAlt: Iran warns US to stay out of Hormuz after Trump says US will ‘guide’ ships

links.headline: Can a peace deal be agreed between Iran and US?
links.excerpt: Both sides want an end to the war but on their terms – and they remain far apart
links.imageAlt: Can a peace deal be agreed between Iran and US?

stories.imageAlt: Iran's top diplomat says a lack of trust is impeding talks to end war with the US

stories.title: Gaza strike targets Hamas armed-wing leader
stories.summary: Israel says it targeted Izz al-Din al-Haddad in Gaza, but his fate remains unclear. Seven Palestinians were reported killed, including a child, with dozens wounded; the framing differs sharply between Israeli-security and humanitarian-focused coverage.

links.headline: Israel targets Hamas leader, kills seven Palestinians
links.excerpt: Seven Palestinians, including a child, have died in Friday's air strikes, which Israel said targeted Hamas militant leader Izz al-Din al-Haddad.
links.imageAlt: Israel targets Hamas leader, kills seven Palestinians

links.headline: Gaza airstrike targeted Hamas military wing leader, Israel says
links.excerpt: Israeli officials say an airstrike in Gaza has targeted Izz al-Din al-Haddad, the leader of Hamas’ military wing. It's unclear if he was killed or injured.
links.imageAlt: Gaza airstrike targeted Hamas military wing leader, Israel says

links.headline: Seven killed in Gaza on Nakba Day as Israel says it targets Hamas member
links.excerpt: Three women and a child among civilians killed in Israel’s latest attacks on Gaza City.
links.imageAlt: Seven killed in Gaza on Nakba Day as Israel says it targets Hamas member

stories.imageAlt: Israel targets Hamas leader, kills seven Palestinians

stories.title: ICC warrant, Senate gunfire and Ronald dela Rosa's escape
stories.summary: This is the major Philippines story to watch: Senator Ronald dela Rosa, Duterte's former police chief, is wanted by the ICC over drug-war killings. Gunfire broke out at the Senate during the attempted arrest drama, and he later fled the building.

links.headline: Gunfire breaks out in Philippine Senate where authorities tried to arrest a senator
links.excerpt: A burst of gunfire rang out in the Philippine Senate, sparking chaos in the building where authorities had tried to arrest a senator wanted by the International Criminal Court in connection with a deadly government crackdown on drugs.
links.imageAlt: Gunfire breaks out in Philippine Senate where authorities tried to arrest a senator

links.headline: ICC unseals arrest warrant for a prominent Philippine senator over drug war killings under Duterte
links.excerpt: The International Criminal Court has unsealed an arrest warrant for a prominent Philippine senator linked to the deadly “war on drugs” overseen by ex-President Rodrigo Duterte, which allegedly involved the extrajudicial killings of suspects.
links.imageAlt: ICC unseals arrest warrant for a prominent Philippine senator over drug war killings under Duterte

links.headline: Philippine politician wanted by ICC flees Senate
links.excerpt: Senate president says Senator Ronald ‘Bato’ dela Rosa ‘no longer in the building’ where he had taken refuge.
links.imageAlt: Philippine politician wanted by ICC flees Senate

links.headline: Timeline: Dela Rosa's May 13 standoff, Senate lockdown and gunfire
links.imageAlt: Timeline: Dela Rosa's May 13 standoff, Senate lockdown and gunfire

stories.imageAlt: Gunfire breaks out in Philippine Senate where authorities tried to arrest a senator

stories.title: Tesla Robotaxi crash details become less redacted
stories.summary: Newly unredacted NHTSA filings show at least two low-speed Tesla Robotaxi crashes involving remote teleoperators. The big question is not just whether autonomy failed, but whether remote intervention is safe and scalable.

links.headline: Tesla discloses new robotaxi crash details
links.excerpt: Tech reporting centered on NHTSA filings, remote teleoperators and autonomy safety questions.
links.imageAlt: Tesla discloses new robotaxi crash details

links.quality: EV specialist outlet with Tesla scrutiny
links.headline: Tesla finally reveals what happened in 17 'Robotaxi' crashes
links.excerpt: Tesla has unredacted all 17 of its ADS crash narratives filed with NHTSA, revealing for the first time what actually happened. Most crashes weren't Tesla's fault — but some are concerning.
links.imageAlt: Tesla finally reveals what happened in 17 'Robotaxi' crashes

links.quality: Tech magazine, safety and regulatory emphasis
links.headline: Tesla Reveals New Details About Robotaxi Crashes—and the Humans Involved
links.excerpt: Remote operators (slowly) drove the automaker’s autonomous vehicles into a metal fence and a construction barricade, Tesla says.
links.imageAlt: Tesla Reveals New Details About Robotaxi Crashes—and the Humans Involved

links.headline: Tesla’s Robotaxi Crash Reports Are Now Unredacted — Here’s What They Show
links.excerpt: Tesla has released unredacted robotaxi crash narratives through April 2026, revealing no major at-fault incidents in Austin despite growing scrutiny over autonomous vehicle safety.
links.imageAlt: Tesla’s Robotaxi Crash Reports Are Now Unredacted — Here’s What They Show

stories.imageAlt: Tesla discloses new robotaxi crash details

stories.title: ChatGPT personal finance preview lets US Pro users connect accounts
stories.summary: OpenAI is previewing a personal finance experience for US Pro users, with Plaid account connections, dashboards and contextual money questions. Useful idea, but the privacy and trust stakes are high because this is financial data.

links.headline: OpenAI previews personal finance in ChatGPT
links.excerpt: Company source describing account connections, finance dashboards and contextual money questions.

links.headline: OpenAI launches ChatGPT for personal finance, will let you connect bank accounts
links.excerpt: Once users connect their accounts, they will see a dashboard of their portfolio performance, spending, subscriptions, and upcoming payments.
links.imageAlt: OpenAI launches ChatGPT for personal finance, will let you connect bank accounts

links.headline: OpenAI rolls out 'ChatGPT Personal Finance' tool with real-time bank account connectivity - The Tech Portal
links.excerpt: OpenAI has rolled out a personal finance tool for ChatGPT that connects directly with users’ financial accounts through Plaid-backed
links.imageAlt: OpenAI rolls out 'ChatGPT Personal Finance' tool with real-time bank account connectivity - The Tech Portal

stories.imageAlt: OpenAI launches ChatGPT for personal finance, will let you connect bank accounts

stories.title: Creative tech and defence pivots: Runway and GoPro
stories.summary: Runway is trying to turn AI video into world models that understand physical reality, while GoPro is exploring defence/aerospace and potential sale options. Together, they show two tech trends worth watching: AI moving beyond text, and civilian imaging hardware chasing defence money.

links.headline: Runway started by helping filmmakers — now it wants to beat Google at AI
links.excerpt: AI video-generation startup Runway is betting that video generation is the path to world models. And that being an AI outsider is an advantage, not a liability.
links.imageAlt: Runway started by helping filmmakers — now it wants to beat Google at AI

links.headline: Even GoPro is pivoting to defense
links.excerpt: The action camera maker, like so many other companies these days, is looking to defense applications as it evaluates a possible sale.
links.imageAlt: Even GoPro is pivoting to defense

links.headline: GoPro founder “fully supports” action camera giant’s potential sale
links.excerpt: GoPro is considering a potential sale, merger, or another strategic move, and founder and CEO Nicholas Woodman says he supports the potential move
links.imageAlt: GoPro founder “fully supports” action camera giant’s potential sale

links.headline: GoPro’s future just took a dramatic turn as defense firms and imaging giants circle the company
links.excerpt: Can GoPro be sold soon?
links.imageAlt: GoPro’s future just took a dramatic turn as defense firms and imaging giants circle the company

stories.imageAlt: Runway started by helping filmmakers — now it wants to beat Google at AI
